seoTitle: How to Undo Changes in Monday.com (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to reverse mistakes in Monday.com using the undo popup, Activity Log, cell history, and Recycle Bin to recover lost data.
intro: Accidental edits and deleted items don't have to mean lost work. This guide covers the different ways Monday.com lets you reverse mistakes, from the instant undo popup that appears after an edit to the Activity Log, cell history, and Recycle Bin for restoring deleted items. It's for anyone who wants to protect their board's data integrity.

steps.heading: Use the instant undo popup
steps.body: Watch for the temporary "Undo" banner that appears at the bottom-left corner immediately after making an edit, and click it to reverse the change.

steps.heading: Check the Board Activity Log
steps.body: Open the Activity Log to view a historical record of every change made by team members on the board.

steps.heading: Revert specific changes from the activity feed
steps.body: Use the activity feed to revert individual column changes, status updates, or accidental text modifications.

steps.heading: Restore deleted items from the Recycle Bin
steps.body: Open the Recycle Bin from your avatar menu to restore entirely deleted items, columns, or boards within 30 days.

steps.heading: Inspect cell history
steps.body: View cell history to see exactly what data was previously typed into a field before it was overwritten.

steps.heading: Set edit permissions to prevent future mistakes
steps.body: Configure board permissions to control who can edit or delete critical data rows going forward.

faq.question: How long do I have to restore a deleted item?
faq.answer: The Recycle Bin lets you restore deleted items, columns, or boards within 30 days of deletion.

faq.question: Can I see who made a specific change?
faq.answer: Yes, the Board Activity Log shows a historical record of every change made by team members.

faq.question: Can I recover a value that was overwritten in a cell?
faq.answer: Yes, you can inspect cell history to see exactly what data was previously entered before it was overwritten.

faq.question: How do I prevent accidental overwrites in the future?
faq.answer: Set up workspace edit permissions to control who can edit or delete critical rows on your board.
